worksheets: Verb Forms – 5 Main Forms (Verbs 51-66)
Analiza y practica las 5 formas principales de los verbos: Base Form, Past Simple, Past Participle, 3rd Person Singular y Gerund.

worksheets.title: Verb Forms – 5 Main Forms (Verbs 51-66)

activities.question: ¿Qué verbos son REGULARES de esta lista?

activities.prompt: Elige 2 verbos de la lista (51-66) que NO hayas practicado aún y escribe sus 5 formas: Base Form, Past Simple, Past Participle, 3rd Person Singular y Gerund. Luego escribe su significado en español. Ejemplo: Learn - Learned - Learned - Learns - Learning = Aprender.

worksheets.description: Analiza y practica las 5 formas principales de los verbos: Base Form, Past Simple, Past Participle, 3rd Person Singular y Gerund.

worksheets: Verb TO BE – Affirmative & Negative (A1)
Practice the verb TO BE with questions, short answers, affirmative and negative forms. Topics: age, nationality, emotions, professions and personal characteristics.

worksheets.title: Verb TO BE – Affirmative & Negative (A1)

activities.text: A: Are you a soccer fan?\nB: Yes, I ____.
A: Is she a doctor?\nB: No, she ____.
I ____ from Mexico. I'm from Guatemala.
Carlos ____ tired. He worked all day.
They ____ at home. They are at school now.
A: Are you hungry?\nB: No, I ____ hungry. I ate ten minutes ago.
She ____ a singer. She sings in a famous band.
We ____ good at math. We prefer English and science.

activities.answer: Are you happy? → Yes, I am.
options: Are you happy? → Yes, I am.
Are you happy? → Yes, am I.
activities.question: Choose the correct SHORT ANSWER.

activities.answer: No, I'm not a doctor. I'm a student.
options: No, I'm not a doctor. I'm a student.
No, I'm a doctor. I'm not a student.
No, I am not doctor. I am student.
activities.question: Read this: 'You are a doctor.' How do you CORRECT it if it's FALSE?
Maria is 12 years old. She is from Guatemala. She is good at sports. Which sentence is TRUE?

activities.title: New Friends
activities.content:
Ana and Carlos are new friends. Ana is 13 years old. She is from Quetzaltenango. She is a student in seventh grade. She is happy today because she is good at English. She isn't tired. She isn't hungry.
    
    Carlos is 14 years old. He is from Guatemala City. He is a student too. He is a soccer fan. He isn't good at English, but he is good at math. He is tired today because he isn't at home. He is at school very early.

activities.prompt: Look at this image of students in a classroom. Write 6 sentences: (1-2) affirmative sentences with 'is' or 'are', (3-4) negative sentences with 'isn't' or 'aren't', (5-6) two questions with 'Is...?' or 'Are...?' about the students in the picture.

Write 6 sentences about yourself. Use the verb TO BE in affirmative and negative. Include: your age, your nationality, your profession (student), one emotion, one thing you are good at, and one thing you are not good at. Then write 2 questions you would ask a new friend.

Interview a partner (real or imaginary). Ask 6 questions using 'Are you...?' (age, nationality, emotions, school, skills, physical state). Answer as your partner would. Then report 4 sentences about your partner using affirmative and negative forms. Example: 'Carlos is 13 years old. He isn't tired. He is a student. He isn't from Mexico.'

worksheets.description: Practice the verb TO BE with questions, short answers, affirmative and negative forms. Topics: age, nationality, emotions, professions and personal characteristics.

worksheets: Modal Verb: MAY – Questions, Wh- Questions & Wishes
Match the English sentences with their Spanish meanings. Practice questions with MAY, Wh- questions with MAY, and wishes with MAY.

worksheet {
  title: "Modal Verb: MAY – Questions, Wh- Questions & Wishes"
  description: "Match the English sentences with their Spanish meanings. Practice questions with MAY, Wh- questions with MAY, and wishes with MAY."

  matching {
    left:
    - "May I go to the bathroom?"
    - "May I borrow your pencil?"
    - "What may I do after class?"
    - "Where may I sit?"
    - "When may we leave?"
    - "Who may help me with this?"
    - "May you have a wonderful birthday!"
    - "May all your dreams come true!"
    - "May you live long and prosper."
    - "May you be very happy together."
    right:
    - "¿Puedo ir al baño?"
    - "¿Me prestas tu lápiz?"
    - "¿Qué puedo hacer después de clase?"
    - "¿Dónde puedo sentarme?"
    - "¿Cuándo podemos irnos?"
    - "¿Quién puede ayudarme con esto?"
    - "¡Que tengas un cumpleaños maravilloso!"
    - "¡Que todos tus sueños se hagan realidad!"
    - "Que tengas una larga y próspera vida."
    - "Que sean muy felices juntos."
  }

  textbox {
    prompt: "Write 2 Wh- questions with MAY that you would ask in a classroom. Then write their Spanish meanings. Example: 'What may I read?' = '¿Qué puedo leer?'"
  }
}

worksheets.title: Modal Verb: MAY – Questions, Wh- Questions & Wishes

left: May I go to the bathroom?
What may I do after class?
Who may help me with this?
May you have a wonderful birthday!
May all your dreams come true!
May you live long and prosper.
May you be very happy together.
right: ¡Que todos tus sueños se hagan realidad!
Que tengas una larga y próspera vida.

activities.prompt: Write 2 Wh- questions with MAY that you would ask in a classroom. Then write their Spanish meanings. Example: 'What may I read?' = '¿Qué puedo leer?'

worksheets.description: Match the English sentences with their Spanish meanings. Practice questions with MAY, Wh- questions with MAY, and wishes with MAY.

worksheets: Verb TO BE – Past Simple: WAS & WERE

worksheets.title: Verb TO BE – Past Simple: WAS & WERE

activities.question: We ____ in Guatemala City last week.
He ____ very tired after the trip.
You ____ late for class this morning.
My friends ____ at the party on Saturday.
Ana and Carlos ____ in the same class last year.

activities.prompt: Escribe 2 oraciones en inglés usando el verbo TO BE en pasado en forma afirmativa (was / were).

worksheets.description: Elige la forma correcta del verbo TO BE en pasado: WAS o WERE.

worksheets: Verb TO BE – Past Simple: Affirmative & Negative
Practica la forma afirmativa (was/were) y negativa (wasn't/weren't) del verbo TO BE en pasado.

worksheets.title: Verb TO BE – Past Simple: Affirmative & Negative

left: They were happy with the result.
He wasn't tired after the trip.
We weren't in Guatemala City last week.

activities.text: I ____ at the party on Saturday. (✓ afirmativo)
They ____ happy with the result. (✗ negativo)
You ____ very kind to help me. (✓ afirmativo)
She ____ at school yesterday. It was a holiday. (✗ negativo)

activities.question: ¿Cuál es la forma NEGATIVA correcta de 'He was tired'?
¿Cuál es la forma AFIRMATIVA correcta para 'ellos estaban felices'?
Completa: 'I ____ hungry, so I ate a sandwich.'

worksheets.description: Practica la forma afirmativa (was/were) y negativa (wasn't/weren't) del verbo TO BE en pasado.

worksheets: Might: Possibilities, Suggestions, and Doubts
Practice the modal verb MIGHT in affirmative and negative forms. Learn to express possibilities, give suggestions, and express doubt.

worksheet {
  title: "Might: Possibilities, Suggestions, and Doubts"
  description: "Practice the modal verb MIGHT in affirmative and negative forms. Learn to express possibilities, give suggestions, and express doubt."

  # EJERCICIO 1: Emparejar uso con ejemplo
  matching {
    left:
    - "Possibility in present or future (Podría)"
    - "Suggestion or advice (Podrías)"
    - "Deny a possibility / doubt (Podría no / Puede que no)"
    right:
    - "It might rain later."
    - "You might try restarting your computer."
    - "She might not like the gift."
  }

  # EJERCICIO 2: Completar estructura afirmativa
  fillblank {
    text: "Complete the affirmative structure:\nSubject / noun + ____ + verb in ____ form + complement."
    answer: "might / base"
  }

  # EJERCICIO 3: Completar estructura negativa
  fillblank {
    text: "Complete the negative structure:\nSubject / noun + might + ____ + verb in base form + complement.\n(Short form: ____)"
    answer: "not / mightn't"
  }

  # EJERCICIO 4: Elegir la opción correcta (uso)
  multiplechoice {
    question: "What is the use of MIGHT in this sentence?\n'You might buy another car, yours is old.'"
    options:
    - "Possibility in the future"
    - "Suggestion or advice"
    - "Denying a possibility"
    - "Expressing doubt"
    answer: "Suggestion or advice"
  }

  # EJERCICIO 5: Completar con MIGHT o MIGHT NOT (afirmativo/negativo)
  fillblank {
    text: "Complete with MIGHT or MIGHT NOT:\n1. She ____ be on time. The traffic is very bad.\n2. They ____ play soccer tomorrow if the weather is nice.\n3. I ____ finish all this food. I'm already full.\n4. He ____ study medicine. He's not sure yet.\n5. We ____ have enough money. The trip is expensive."
    answer: "might not / might / might not / might / might not"
  }

  # EJERCICIO 6: Seleccionar la traducción correcta
  multiplechoice {
    question: "What is the correct Spanish translation of:\n'He might not get a girlfriend.'"
    options:
    - "Él podría tener una novia."
    - "Él no podría tener una novia."
    - "Él podría no tener una novia."
    - "Él tendrá una novia."
    answer: "Él podría no tener una novia."
  }

  # EJERCICIO 7: Identificar si es posibilidad, sugerencia o duda
  multiplechoice {
    question: "Identify the use of MIGHT in:\n'She might clean her room before the party starts.'"
    options:
    - "Possibility"
    - "Suggestion (advice)"
    - "Doubt / denial"
    - "Permission"
    answer: "Suggestion (advice)"
  }

  # EJERCICIO 8: Completar oraciones con la forma correcta
  fillblank {
    text: "Complete each sentence with MIGHT or MIGHT NOT + the verb in parentheses:\n1. They ____ (be) at home now. Let's call them.\n2. You ____ (try) drinking water. It helps with headaches.\n3. The teacher ____ (give) a quiz tomorrow. Nobody knows.\n4. I ____ (attend) the meeting. I feel very sick.\n5. She ____ (win) the contest with that dress. It's not very pretty."
    answer: "might be / might try / might give / might not attend / might not win"
  }

  # EJERCICIO 9: Seleccionar la oración correcta (estructura)
  multiplechoice {
    question: "Which sentence has the CORRECT structure with MIGHT?"
    options:
    - "She might to go to the party."
    - "He might goes to the concert."
    - "They might not be waiting outside."
    - "We mightn't to have time."
    answer: "They might not be waiting outside."
  }

  # EJERCICIO 10: Emparejar oración con su traducción
  matching {
    left:
    - "It might rain tomorrow."
    - "You might think about your family."
    - "I might not see him again."
    right:
    - "Podría llover mañana."
    - "Podrías pensar en tu familia."
    - "Podría no verlo otra vez."
  }
}

worksheets.title: Might: Possibilities, Suggestions, and Doubts

left: Deny a possibility / doubt (Podría no / Puede que no)
right: You might try restarting your computer.
She might not like the gift.

activities.text: Complete the affirmative structure:\nSubject / noun + ____ + verb in ____ form + complement.
Complete the negative structure:\nSubject / noun + might + ____ + verb in base form + complement.\n(Short form: ____)

activities.question: What is the use of MIGHT in this sentence?\n'You might buy another car, yours is old.'

activities.text:
Complete with MIGHT or MIGHT NOT:
1. She ____ be on time. The traffic is very bad.
2. They ____ play soccer tomorrow if the weather is nice.
3. I ____ finish all this food. I'm already full.
4. He ____ study medicine. He's not sure yet.
5. We ____ have enough money. The trip is expensive.
activities.answer: might not / might / might not / might / might not
Él podría no tener una novia.
options: Él no podría tener una novia.
Él podría no tener una novia.
activities.question: What is the correct Spanish translation of:\n'He might not get a girlfriend.'
Identify the use of MIGHT in:\n'She might clean her room before the party starts.'

activities.text:
Complete each sentence with MIGHT or MIGHT NOT + the verb in parentheses:
1. They ____ (be) at home now. Let's call them.
2. You ____ (try) drinking water. It helps with headaches.
3. The teacher ____ (give) a quiz tomorrow. Nobody knows.
4. I ____ (attend) the meeting. I feel very sick.
5. She ____ (win) the contest with that dress. It's not very pretty.
activities.answer: might be / might try / might give / might not attend / might not win
They might not be waiting outside.
options: She might to go to the party.
He might goes to the concert.
They might not be waiting outside.
activities.question: Which sentence has the CORRECT structure with MIGHT?

left: You might think about your family.
I might not see him again.

worksheets.description: Practice the modal verb MIGHT in affirmative and negative forms. Learn to express possibilities, give suggestions, and express doubt.

Write 2 Wh- questions with MAY that you would ask in a classroom. Then write their Spanish meanings. Example: 'What may I read?' = '¿Qué puedo leer?'
responses.student_answer:
What may i buy this afternoon? Qué puedo comprar esta tarde?
Where may i go? A dónde puedo ir?

responses: Julian Armando Jose De La Roca Chávez

responses.prompt: I ____ at home yesterday.
They ____ happy with the result.
She ____ a teacher before.
We ____ in Guatemala City last week.
He ____ very tired after the trip.
You ____ late for class this morning.
The movie ____ really interesting.
My friends ____ at the party on Saturday.
It ____ cold and rainy yesterday.
Ana and Carlos ____ in the same class last year.
Escribe 2 oraciones en inglés usando el verbo TO BE en pasado en forma afirmativa (was / were).
I was at home yesterday.
She was a teacher before.
They were happy with the result.
He wasn't tired after the trip.
We weren't in Guatemala City last week.
You weren't late for class.
I ____ at the party on Saturday. (✓ afirmativo)
They ____ happy with the result. (✗ negativo)
You ____ very kind to help me. (✓ afirmativo)
She ____ at school yesterday. It was a holiday. (✗ negativo)
¿Cuál es la forma NEGATIVA correcta de 'He was tired'?
¿Cuál es la forma AFIRMATIVA correcta para 'ellos estaban felices'?
Completa: 'I ____ hungry, so I ate a sandwich.'

responses.prompt: Possibility in present or future (Podría)
Suggestion or advice (Podrías)

responses.student_answer: You might try restarting your computer.

responses.prompt: Deny a possibility / doubt (Podría no / Puede que no)

responses.student_answer: She might not like the gift.

responses.prompt: Complete the affirmative structure:\nSubject / noun + ____ + verb in ____ form + complement.
Complete the negative structure:\nSubject / noun + might + ____ + verb in base form + complement.\n(Short form: ____)
What is the use of MIGHT in this sentence?\n'You might buy another car, yours is old.'

Complete with MIGHT or MIGHT NOT:
1. She ____ be on time. The traffic is very bad.
2. They ____ play soccer tomorrow if the weather is nice.
3. I ____ finish all this food. I'm already full.
4. He ____ study medicine. He's not sure yet.
5. We ____ have enough money. The trip is expensive.
responses.correct_answer: might not / might / might not / might / might not

responses.prompt: What is the correct Spanish translation of:\n'He might not get a girlfriend.'
responses.correct_answer: Él podría no tener una novia.
responses.student_answer: Él podría no tener una novia.

responses.prompt: Identify the use of MIGHT in:\n'She might clean her room before the party starts.'

Complete each sentence with MIGHT or MIGHT NOT + the verb in parentheses:
1. They ____ (be) at home now. Let's call them.
2. You ____ (try) drinking water. It helps with headaches.
3. The teacher ____ (give) a quiz tomorrow. Nobody knows.
4. I ____ (attend) the meeting. I feel very sick.
5. She ____ (win) the contest with that dress. It's not very pretty.
responses.correct_answer: might be / might try / might give / might not attend / might not win

responses.prompt: Which sentence has the CORRECT structure with MIGHT?
responses.correct_answer: They might not be waiting outside.
responses.student_answer: They might not be waiting outside.
